displayTitle: Predict It Before You Burn Film

beats.narration: Welcome to Level Two. You already know what photons do in steel. Now you'll predict what a technique does before you burn film.
Three events: photoelectric absorption makes contrast. Compton scattering, dominant at gamma energies, makes fog. Pair production converts to matter above 1.022 mega electron volts.
Together, that's attenuation: each identical layer removes the same fraction. About half an inch of steel halves an iridium beam: four layers, one sixteenth.
Distance works by squares: double it, intensity falls to one quarter. 240 milliroentgen per hour at 1 meter? About 15 at 4 meters.

payload.heading: Intensity falls with distance squared
payload.note: DOUBLE THE DISTANCE → ONE QUARTER THE INTENSITY

beats.narration: Three tools, every working day: decay, inverse square, half value layers. And the survey meter always gets the final word.

payload.sub: And the survey meter has the final word.
